s.t: How is our contact center performing right now?

layers.nm: Engineering's Thursday spike was caused by a data

s.t: The Collections queue handled an estimated 2.208M outbound calls this month through Sestek Knovvuvoice automation. Thursday's run created the week's 170K peak. Smarter intent routing makes an estimated 60% of those live-agent transfers avoidable.

layers.nm: Marketing's Claude Sonnet 4.6 adoption grew 8- WoW

s.t: Support handled 1,638 interactions this week across 50 active agents. At 142 calls per agent each day, its monthly volume is 7,100, or 0.2% of total contact center volume this week.

layers.nm: Marketing spend grew 8- month over month

s.t: Support automation grew 8% month over month

layers.nm: Marketing's shift from GPT-4o to Claude Sonnet 4.6

s.t: The Support team's shift to Sestek Knovvu virtual agents has been cost-neutral per contact, while resolutionquality improved in the latest review. The team is piloting automated call summaries for high-volume queues.

layers.nm: , driven primarily by Engineering's Claude Opus 4.

s.t: Your interaction volume rose 23% week over week, driven by the Support team's peak on Thursday of 170K calls. Automated containment grew 8% month over month. Your self-service hit rate improved to 38.6%, and there are 3 savings opportunities worth $27.8K per month.

layers.nm: $884K spent this week, averaging $126K per day

s.t: 884K interactions this week, averaging 126K per day

layers.nm: This week saw combined AI spend of $5,025 across O

s.t: Combined interactions across voice and digital channels rose 23% from 779K the prior week. Calls and chat accounted for 89% of weekly volume. Two days set new all-time highs, including Thursday's 170K peak.
